steps.title: Spot the key contrast
steps.content: The passage uses "but" to set up a contradiction: the basking shark is classified as a "full ectotherm," yet its body temperature is elevated above the ambient water temperature. The blank must name the relationship between this finding and the classification.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean incompatible with or at odds with its classification. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — incongruous with: not in harmony or incompatible — an elevated body temperature is incompatible with being a "full ectotherm," which by definition matches ambient temperature. ✓
  • Choice B — subordinate to: lower in rank or secondary to — this implies a hierarchy between the finding and classification, not a contradiction. ✗
  • Choice C — opportune for: well-timed or advantageous for — the finding doesn't benefit or suit the classification; it conflicts with it. ✗
  • Choice D — indicative of: serving as a sign of — an elevated temperature would indicate endothermy, not confirm the ectotherm classification. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
